extraction.one_line_summary: GODON David won from the breakaway after SOLAIRE Marc bridged solo from the peloton two climbs earlier, holding off GC riders who only attacked inside final 200 meters.
tactical_patterns: SOLAIRE Marc bridged alone from peloton to breakaway two climbs before summit, demonstrating GC riders let breakaway threats escape too late.
GC favorites waited until final 200m to attack on 16.5km climb, revealing conservatism before Angliru enabled breakaway to succeed.
VLASOV Aleksandr allowed to gap by ROGLIČ teammates during climb because 6:52 GC deficit made him irrelevant to podium battle.
GODON needed to attack SOLAIRE earlier on final climb but waited for closing sprint, nearly losing to superior climber's finishing speed.

rider_signals.GODON David: positive: won first Grand Tour stage by holding off SOLAIRE in sprint after long breakaway, showing elite climbing endurance
rider_signals.SOLER Marc: positive: bridged solo from peloton to breakaway group over multiple climbs, then nearly won stage despite towing GODON
rider_signals.MARTIN Dan: positive: initiated GC attacks with 200m remaining on brutal finish, showing aggressive intent unlike other favorites
rider_signals.VLASOV Aleksandr: neutral: allowed gap by ROGLIČ team due to 6:52 deficit, confirming his irrelevance to podium despite improving form
rider_signals.STORER Michael: positive: third on stage with teammate DONOVAN fifth, Sunweb placing two riders top-5 from breakaway
rider_signals.MARTIN Guillaume: positive: extended KOM jersey lead despite finishing minute down, prioritizing points over stage position
rider_signals.BENNETT George: negative: finished 19th at 1+ minute despite pacing for JUMBO, showing weakness compared to other domestiques on steep gradients
